issue.author: zhendongsu

comments.author: EKCKABATOP54
comments.body: Isn't that ub? https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/ub ; Infinite loop without side-effects

comments.author: zhendongsu
comments.body:
> Isn't that ub? https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/ub ; Infinite loop without side-effects

It's not an infinite loop; the body of the outer loop will be executed once and return 1.

comments.author: efriedma-quic
comments.body: I think 99d258216 broke this; it attaches incorrect nsw/nuw flags on SCEV expressions in loop conditions. CC @wsmoses @LebedevRI @preames

comments.author: luxufan
comments.body: I have tried to fix this and posted a patch https://reviews.llvm.org/D144861, but I am not sure if it is the right fix.

issue.title: [SCEV] wrong code at -O2 on x86_64-linux_gnu

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare void @non_exit_use(i32)

; Function Attrs: willreturn
define void @SLE(i32 %len) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ %inc, %for.body ], [ 0, %entry ]
  call void @non_exit_use(i32 %iv) #1
  %inc = add i32 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp sle i32 %iv, %len
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { willreturn }
attributes #1 = { nounwind willreturn }

target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare void @non_exit_use(i32)

; Function Attrs: willreturn
define void @ULE(i32 %len) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ %inc, %for.body ], [ 0, %entry ]
  call void @non_exit_use(i32 %iv) #1
  %inc = add i32 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp ule i32 %iv, %len
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { willreturn }
attributes #1 = { nounwind willreturn }

target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare void @non_exit_use(i32)

; Function Attrs: willreturn
define void @SGE(i32 %end) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ %inc, %for.body ], [ 100, %entry ]
  call void @non_exit_use(i32 %iv) #1
  %inc = add i32 %iv, -1
  %cmp = icmp sge i32 %iv, %end
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { willreturn }
attributes #1 = { nounwind willreturn }

target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

; Function Attrs: mustprogress
define void @pr54191(i64 %n) #0 {
entry:
  %guard = icmp sgt i64 %n, 1
  br i1 %guard, label %loop, label %exit

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 2, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 2
  %cmp = icmp sle i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { mustprogress }

@x = external global i32

; Function Attrs: mustprogress
define i32 @main(i32 %iv.start, i32 %arg2) #0 {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ %iv.start, %entry ]
  br i1 false, label %loop2.preheader, label %loop.latch

loop2.preheader:                                  ; preds = %loop
  br label %loop2

loop2:                                            ; preds = %loop2, %loop2.preheader
  %x = load i32, ptr @x, align 4
  %cmp1 = icmp ult i32 %iv, %x
  br i1 %cmp1, label %loop2.exit, label %loop2

loop2.exit:                                       ; preds = %loop2
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop2.exit, %loop
  %iv.next = sdiv i32 %arg2, %iv
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i32 %iv, -1
  br i1 %cmp2, label %loop.exit, label %loop

loop.exit:                                        ; preds = %loop.latch
  %iv.next.lcssa = phi i32 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  ret i32 %iv.next.lcssa
}

attributes #0 = { mustprogress }

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define i32 @test(i1 %c.0, i32 %m) {
entry:
  br label %outer.header

outer.header:                                     ; preds = %outer.latch, %entry
  %iv.1 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.1.next, %outer.latch ]
  %iv.2 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.2.next, %outer.latch ]
  %max.0 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %max.1, %outer.latch ]
  %0 = sext i32 %iv.1 to i64
  br label %inner.1

inner.1:                                          ; preds = %inner.1, %outer.header
  %c.1 = icmp slt i64 0, %0
  br i1 %c.1, label %inner.1, label %inner.2.header

inner.2.header:                                   ; preds = %inner.2.latch, %inner.1
  %iv.3 = phi i32 [ 0, %inner.1 ], [ %iv.3.next, %inner.2.latch ]
  br i1 %c.0, label %outer.latch, label %inner.2.latch

inner.2.latch:                                    ; preds = %inner.2.header
  %iv.3.next = add i32 %iv.3, 1
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i32 %iv.3, %iv.2
  br i1 %c.2, label %outer.latch, label %inner.2.header

outer.latch:                                      ; preds = %inner.2.latch, %inner.2.header
  %max.1 = phi i32 [ %m, %inner.2.latch ], [ %iv.3, %inner.2.header ]
  %iv.1.next = add i32 %iv.1, 1
  %iv.2.next = add i32 %iv.2, 1
  %c.3 = icmp ugt i32 %iv.2, %max.0
  br i1 %c.3, label %exit, label %outer.header, !llvm.loop !0

exit:                                             ; preds = %outer.latch
  ret i32 0
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.mustprogress"}

; Function Attrs: mustprogress
define i16 @pr57336(i16 %end, i16 %m) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %inc8 = phi i16 [ %inc, %for.body ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %inc137 = phi i32 [ %inc1, %for.body ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %inc1 = add nsw i32 %inc137, 1
  %inc = add nsw i16 %inc8, 1
  %mul = mul nsw i16 %m, %inc8
  %cmp.not = icmp slt i16 %end, %mul
  br i1 %cmp.not, label %crit_edge, label %for.body

crit_edge:                                        ; preds = %for.body
  %inc137.lcssa = phi i32 [ %inc137, %for.body ]
  %conv = trunc i32 %inc137.lcssa to i16
  ret i16 %conv
}

attributes #0 = { mustprogress }
